configuration.name: Ariane 62

manufacturer.name: Arianespace
manufacturer.description: Arianespace SA is a multinational company founded in 1980 as the world's first commercial launch service provider. It undertakes the production, operation, and marketing of the Ariane programme. Their vehicles launch exclusively from French Guiana in South America.

manufacturer.name: ArianeGroup
manufacturer.description: ArianeGroup (formerly Airbus Safran Launchers) is an aerospace company based in France. A joint venture between Airbus and Safran, the company was founded in 2015 and is headquartered in Issy-les-Moulineaux. It consists of three core arms: aerospace, defence and security.

configuration.description: The Ariane 6 family is currently under development by the European Space Agency, with two variations including the Ariane 62 with two solid boosters intended for government and scientific missions.

mission.name: Metop-SG A2
mission.description: Third of EUMETSAT's second generation of Metop weather satellites.

pad.name: Ariane Launch Area 4

image.name: ELA-4
image.credit: ESA/CNES/Arianespace/Optique Vidéo du CSG - P. PIRON

pad.description: ELA-4, is a launch pad and associated facilities at the Centre Spatial Guyanais in French Guiana. The complex is composed of a launch pad with mobile gantry, an horizontal assembly building and a dedicated launch operations building. ELA-4 is operated by Arianespace as part of the Ariane 6 program.

location.name: Guiana Space Centre, French Guiana

celestial_body.name: Earth
celestial_body.description: Earth is the third planet from the Sun and the only astronomical object known to harbor life.

location.description: The Guiana Space Centre is a European spaceport to the northwest of Kourou in French Guiana, a region of France in South America. Kourou is located at a latitude of 5°. In operation since 1968, it is a suitable location for a spaceport because of its equatorial location and open sea to the east.

image.name: Vega & Ariane 5 pads at the Guiana Space Centre

launch_service_provider.name: Arianespace
launch_service_provider.description: Arianespace SA is a multinational company founded in 1980 as the world's first commercial launch service provider. It undertakes the production, operation, and marketing of the Ariane programme. Their vehicles launch exclusively from French Guiana in South America.

program.name: Copernicus
program.description: Copernicus is the European Union's Earth observation programme coordinated and managed for the European Commission by the European Union Agency for the Space Programme in partnership with the European Space Agency (ESA), the EU Member States. It aims at achieving a global, continuous, autonomous, high quality, wide range Earth observation capacity. Providing accurate, timely and easily accessible information to, among other things, improve the management of the environment, understand and mitigate the effects of climate change, and ensure civil security.

agencies.name: European Union Agency for the Space Programme
